format: FIFA World Cup 2026 knockout bracket — Round of 32 slot structure, third-place seeding rule, and fixed bracket progression path
thirdPlaceRule: The 8 best third-placed teams are first RANKED across all 12 groups using FIFA's group-stage criteria (Article 13 of the official tournament regulations), in this order: (1) points; (2) goal difference; (3) goals scored; (4) team conduct/disciplinary (fair-play) score from yellow/red cards; (5) FIFA/Coca-Cola Men's World Ranking. (Head-to-head does NOT apply because the teams are from different groups. Drawing of lots is no longer the final tiebreak for 2026 — the FIFA World Ranking is.) The 8 qualifying thirds do NOT fall into fixed slots based on rank. Instead, FIFA uses a PRE-DETERMINED MAPPING TABLE (Annex C of the tournament regulations) that lists all 495 possible combinations (12 choose 8 = 495) of which 8 of the 12 groups supply a qualifying third-placed team, and for each combination assigns those eight thirds to the eight designated Round of 32 slots. The eight group winners that face a third-placed team are the winners of Groups A, C, D, E, G, I, K and L (matches 74, 77, 79, 80, 81, 82, 85, 87). Each such slot draws its third-placed team from a fixed pool of five specified groups, e.g. Match 74 = '3rd from Group A/B/C/D/F', Match 79 = '3rd from Group C/E/F/H/I'. The Annex C table is engineered so that, whichever 8 groups actually supply a qualifying third, each of the eight slots receives exactly one of them with no group facing its own runner-up/winner inappropriately.
progression: FIXED BRACKET PATH (match numbers per official FIFA schedule). ROUND OF 16 (matches 89-96): M89 = W74 v W77 (Jul 4, Lincoln Financial Field, Philadelphia); M90 = W73 v W75 (Jul 4, NRG Stadium, Houston); M91 = W76 v W78 (Jul 5, MetLife Stadium, East Rutherford); M92 = W79 v W80 (Jul 5, Estadio Azteca, Mexico City); M93 = W83 v W84 (Jul 6, AT&T Stadium, Arlington); M94 = W81 v W82 (Jul 6, Lumen Field, Seattle); M95 = W86 v W88 (Jul 7, Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Atlanta); M96 = W85 v W87 (Jul 7, BC Place, Vancouver). QUARTERFINALS (97-100): M97 = W89 v W90 (Jul 9, Gillette Stadium, Foxborough); M98 = W93 v W94 (Jul 10, SoFi Stadium, Inglewood); M99 = W91 v W92 (Jul 11, Hard Rock Stadium, Miami Gardens); M100 = W95 v W96 (Jul 11, Arrowhead Stadium, Kansas City). SEMIFINALS (101-102): M101 = W97 v W98 (Jul 14, AT&T Stadium, Arlington); M102 = W99 v W100 (Jul 15, Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Atlanta). THIRD-PLACE PLAY-OFF: M103 = L101 v L102 (Jul 18, Hard Rock Stadium, Miami Gardens). FINAL: M104 = W101 v W102 (Jul 19, MetLife Stadium, East Rutherford). So a Round-of-32 winner's path to the final is: R32 (73-88) -> R16 (89-96) -> QF (97-100) -> SF (101/102) -> Final (104), per the wiring above.

r32.venue: MetLife Stadium, East Rutherford (New York/New Jersey)
Levi's Stadium, Santa Clara (San Francisco Bay Area)

notes:
FORMAT: 48 teams, 12 groups (A-L). 32 qualifiers = 12 group winners + 12 runners-up + 8 best third-placed teams. Single-elimination from Round of 32 (matches 73-88), played 28 Jun - 3 Jul 2026.

SOURCE CONSISTENCY: The R32 slot structure, full bracket wiring (R16->QF->SF->Final), venues and match numbers are CONFIRMED and consistent across Wikipedia, ESPN and Sky Sports. Kickoff times are reported by sources in local time / UTC offsets; I converted each to UTC. Sources give offsets as: Pacific (UTC-7, PDT), Mountain/Central-Mexico Monterrey (UTC-6, listed by ESPN as 'UTC-6' for Estadio BBVA), Central (UTC-5), Eastern (UTC-4), and Mexico City (UTC-6 CST, no DST). Note Mexico City is on UTC-6 year-round; ESPN listed Azteca matches as 'UTC-6', which I used. Minor uncertainty (+/- 1 hr) is possible on a few cross-border kickoffs due to DST/offset labeling in sources, but all dates and the local clock times are confirmed.

RESOLVED DISCREPANCY: One ESPN extraction initially showed Match 82's third-place pool as 'A/H/I/J' (4 groups); Sky Sports, a second ESPN read, and a direct search all confirm it is 'A/E/H/I/J' (5 groups), matching the pattern that every third-place slot draws from exactly five groups. I used 'A/E/H/I/J'.

TEAM NAMES NOT INCLUDED: Per the honesty rule, I did NOT fill any slot with actual team names. Some sources interleave specific nations (e.g., 'Mexico' as Group A winner, 'USA' as Group D winner, 'Brazil' as Group C winner, 'Germany' as Group E winner, 'Argentina' as Group J winner) reflecting in-progress standings, and one source (CBS) showed clearly hypothetical/projected matchups that conflicted with others. Since group standings as of 25 Jun 2026 could not be definitively confirmed match-by-match, all slots are given as structural definitions (Winner/Runner-up/Third Place of group X) only.

ANNEX C: The exact 495-row mapping table (which specific qualifying-group combination sends which third to which slot) is in FIFA's official tournament regulations (Annex C) and was not reproduced in full by the public sources fetched; the five-group pool per slot listed above is the published summary of that table.
